title: English/Preserve Meter
prompt:
You are preparing a bilingual reading edition for Kesh Temple Hymn. Translate to modern english.

Split the source chapter into paired bilingual chunks that preserve all source content. Return JSON only with this shape: {"chapterTitle": string, "notes": string, "chunks": [{"originalText": string, "translatedText": string, "type": "verse" | "prose"}]}. The concatenation of all originalText values must exactly reproduce the source chapter without dropping or duplicating content.

e2 1-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
nam2-nun-e nam2-nun-e e2-ta nam-ta-ab-ed2
den-lil2 nam2-nun-e e2-ta nam-ta-ab-ed2
nam2-nun-e nam-lugal-la e2-ta nam-ta-ab-ed2
chunks.translatedText:
The princely one, the princely one, from the house came forth,
Enlil, the princely one, from the house came forth,
The princely one, in kingship, from the house came forth.

chunks.originalText:
den-lil2 kur-kur-ra igi mi-ni-in-il2-il2-i
den-lil2-ra kur ni2-ba mu-na-il2-il2-i
chunks.translatedText:
Enlil upon the lands his eyes did lift and gaze,
To Enlil all the lands their own heads lift and raise.

chunks.originalText:
an ub-da 4 den-lil2-ra jickiri6-gin7 mu-na-sig7
kecki saj il2 mu-na-ni-in-jal2
chunks.translatedText:
The four corners of heaven for Enlil like a garden grew green,
And Kesh with its head held high was a sight to be seen.

chunks.originalText:
kecki kur-kur-ra saj il2-bi
den-lil2-le kecki za3-mi2 am3-ma-ab-be2
chunks.translatedText:
Kesh, among the lands, its head is lifted high,
Enlil for Kesh a song of praise did then glorify.

chunks.originalText:
dnisaba nu-ka-ac-bi-im
inim-bi-ta sa-gin7 im-da-an-sur
chunks.translatedText:
Nisaba was its arbiter, its word and its guide,
With those words, like a net, she wove them side by side.

chunks.originalText:
dub-ba sar-sar cu-ce3 al-ja2-ja2
e2 muc3 kalam-ma gud huc aratta
e2 kecki muc3 kalam-ma gud huc aratta
chunks.translatedText:
Written on the tablets, held within the hand,
House, the jewel of the land, fierce bull of Aratta's land,
House of Kesh, the jewel of the land, fierce bull of Aratta's land.

chunks.originalText:
hur-saj-da mu2-a an-da gu2 la2-a
e2-kur-da mu2-a kur-ra saj il2-bi
chunks.translatedText:
Grown with the mountain, reaching to the sky,
Grown with the Ekur, lifting its head on high.

chunks.originalText:
abzu-gin7 ri-a hur-saj-gin7 sig7-sig7-ga
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
chunks.translatedText:
Founded like the Abzu, green like the mountain range,
Will any man bring forth a thing so great and strange?

chunks.originalText:
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 1-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The first house.

chapters.notes: The opening of the Kesh Temple Hymn describes the divine origins of the temple, its praise by Enlil, and its architectural majesty, comparing it to the Abzu and the mountains.

chapters.title: House 2
chapters.sourceText:
e2 dug3 ki dug3-ga du3-a
e2 kecki ki dug3-ga du3-a
ma2-gur8 nun-gin7 an-na dirig-ga
ma2-gur8 kug-gin7 ka2 si ri-a
ma2 an-na-gin7 muc3 kur-kur-ra

nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8

chunks.originalText:
e2 dug3 ki dug3-ga du3-a
e2 kecki ki dug3-ga du3-a
chunks.translatedText:
Good house, built in a place so good and fair,
Kesh house, built in a place so good and fair.

chunks.originalText:
ma2-gur8 nun-gin7 an-na dirig-ga
ma2-gur8 kug-gin7 ka2 si ri-a
ma2 an-na-gin7 muc3 kur-kur-ra
u5 ma2 ban3-da-gin7 pec10-ta sur-sur-ra
chunks.translatedText:
Like a princely barge, it floats upon the sky,
Like a holy barge, with horns it sails on high.
Like the boat of heaven, the jewel of the land,
Like a cabin boat, it glides from off the strand.

chunks.originalText:
e2 gud-gin7 ur5 ca4 ninda2-gin7 gu3 nun di
e2 cag4-bi-ta lipic kalam-ma
a-ga-bi-ta zi ki-en-gi-ra
chunks.translatedText:
House like a lowing bull, like a bull that bellows loud,
Within its heart, the land's own pulse is proud.
From its rear, the life of Sumer is endowed.

chunks.originalText:
e2 eb gal an-ne2 us2-sa
e2 zid-da gal an-ne2 us2-sa
e2 men gal an-ne2 us2-sa
e2 dtir-an-na an-ne2 us2-sa
chunks.translatedText:
Great shrine of the heavens, reaching to the sky,
Great righteous house, reaching to the sky,
Great crown of a house, reaching to the sky,
House of the rainbow, reaching to the sky.

chunks.originalText:
e2 muc3-bi an cag4-ga la2-a
te-me-bi abzu-a sig9-ga
jissu-bi kur-kur-ra dul-la
chunks.translatedText:
Its jewel in heaven's heart, a wondrous view,
Its foundations in the Abzu deep are laid,
Its shadow over all the lands is displayed.

chunks.originalText:
e2 an-ne2 ki jar-ra den-lil2-le za3-mi2 dug4-ga
ama dnin-tu-ra ec-bar kij2 dug4-ga
e2 kecki gurun-na sig7-ga
chunks.translatedText:
House founded by An, by Enlil praised in song,
For Mother Nintu, where the oracles belong.
House of Kesh, with fruit it grows so green.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 2-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The second house.

chapters.notes: The second house of the Kesh Temple Hymn describes the temple's physical and spiritual grandeur using maritime and celestial imagery, emphasizing its foundation in the Abzu and its reach toward the heavens.

chapters.title: House 3
chapters.sourceText:
e2 an-ce3 10 car2iku ki-ce3 5 car2iku
e2 an-ce3 10 bur3iku ki-ce3 5 bur3iku
e2 an-ce3 alim ki-ce3 lu-lim
e2 an-ce3 ceg9-bar ki-ce3 tarah-mac
e2 an-ce3 ceg9-bar gun3-a ki-ce3 tarah-mac sig7-ga
e2 an-ce3 muc mir-re-gin7 sig7-ga ki-ce3 u5mucen-gin7 a-e cu2-a
e2 an-ce3 ud-gin7 ed2-a ki-ce3 iti6-gin7 barag2-ga
e2 an-ce3 cita2 cul ki-ce3 jictun3-am3
e2 an-ce3 kur-ra-am3 ki-ce3 idim-ma-am3
e2 an-ce3 3-kam-bi na-nam
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-an-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 3-kam-ma

originalDocument.fullText:

e2 an-ce3 ceg9-bar gun3-a ki-ce3 tarah-mac sig7-ga
e2 an-ce3 muc mir-re-gin7 sig7-ga ki-ce3 u5mucen-gin7 a-e cu2-a
e2 an-ce3 ud-gin7 ed2-a ki-ce3 iti6-gin7 barag2-ga
e2 an-ce3 cita2 cul ki-ce3 jictun3-am3
e2 an-ce3 kur-ra-am3 ki-ce3 idim-ma-am3
e2 an-ce3 3-kam-bi na-nam
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-an-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 3-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
e2 an-ce3 10 car2iku ki-ce3 5 car2iku
e2 an-ce3 10 bur3iku ki-ce3 5 bur3iku
chunks.translatedText:
Ten units high to heaven, five units deep in earth,
Ten measures high to heaven, five measures deep in earth.

chunks.originalText:
e2 an-ce3 alim ki-ce3 lu-lim
e2 an-ce3 ceg9-bar ki-ce3 tarah-mac
e2 an-ce3 ceg9-bar gun3-a ki-ce3 tarah-mac sig7-ga
chunks.translatedText:
A bison for the heavens, a stag for earth below,
A wild ram for the heavens, a goat for earth below,
A dappled ram for heaven, a fair goat for the earth.

chunks.originalText:
e2 an-ce3 muc mir-re-gin7 sig7-ga ki-ce3 u5mucen-gin7 a-e cu2-a
e2 an-ce3 ud-gin7 ed2-a ki-ce3 iti6-gin7 barag2-ga
chunks.translatedText:
Like fierce snakes in the heavens, like ducks on water's berth,
Like sun that rises heavenward, like moon on earth outspread.

chunks.originalText:
e2 an-ce3 cita2 cul ki-ce3 jictun3-am3
e2 an-ce3 kur-ra-am3 ki-ce3 idim-ma-am3
chunks.translatedText:
A hero's mace for heaven, an axe for earth's own bed,
A mountain for the heavens, a spring for earth's own floor.

chunks.originalText: e2 an-ce3 3-kam-bi na-nam
chunks.translatedText: The house for the third time is truly this and more.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-an-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 3-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?
The third house.

chapters.notes: This section emphasizes the cosmic dimensions and dual nature of the temple, contrasting its heavenly height with its earthly depth using animal, celestial, and elemental metaphors.

chapters.title: House 4
chapters.sourceText:
iriki ga-am3 iriki ga-am3 cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 kecki iriki ga-am3 cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
cag4-bi ur-saj-ur-saj-e-ne si mu-un-si-sa2-e-ne
ec-bar kij2 dug4-ga cu gal mu-un-du7-du7
e2 gud du7-du7-dam gu2 am3-ma-gur-re
e2 gud car2-a al-gu7-e
e2 udu car2-a-am3 al-gu7-e
X X-ma-am3 gu2-bi luh-luh-ha
barag-barag-e-ne gu2-ne am3-ma-jal2-le-ne
jictaskarin-da men an-da-gur3-ru
jicasal2-gin7 an-da-tal2-tal2 sal-la
hur-saj-da an-da-sig7-sig7-ga
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-an-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 4-kam-ma

originalDocument.fullText:
iriki ga-am3 iriki ga-am3 cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 kecki iriki ga-am3 cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
cag4-bi ur-saj-ur-saj-e-ne si mu-un-si-sa2-e-ne
ec-bar kij2 dug4-ga cu gal mu-un-du7-du7
e2 gud du7-du7-dam gu2 am3-ma-gur-re
e2 gud car2-a al-gu7-e
e2 udu car2-a-am3 al-gu7-e

barag-barag-e-ne gu2-ne am3-ma-jal2-le-ne
jictaskarin-da men an-da-gur3-ru
jicasal2-gin7 an-da-tal2-tal2 sal-la
hur-saj-da an-da-sig7-sig7-ga
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-an-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 4-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
iriki ga-am3 iriki ga-am3 cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 kecki iriki ga-am3 cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
chunks.translatedText:
A city, oh, a city! Who knows what lies within?
The house of Kesh, a city! Who knows what lies within?

chunks.originalText:
cag4-bi ur-saj-ur-saj-e-ne si mu-un-si-sa2-e-ne
ec-bar kij2 dug4-ga cu gal mu-un-du7-du7
chunks.translatedText:
Within its walls the heroes set all things in their place,
Decisions made and labors done with perfect, holy grace.

chunks.originalText:
e2 gud du7-du7-dam gu2 am3-ma-gur-re
e2 gud car2-a al-gu7-e
e2 udu car2-a-am3 al-gu7-e
chunks.translatedText:
The house is like a perfect bull that bellows loud and deep,
It eats a myriad of bulls, a harvest it shall keep,
It eats a myriad of rams, and flocks of grazing sheep.

chunks.originalText:
X X-ma-am3 gu2-bi luh-luh-ha
barag-barag-e-ne gu2-ne am3-ma-jal2-le-ne
chunks.translatedText:
Its neck is washed and purified within the sacred hall,
The rulers bow their heads to it, the masters of them all.

chunks.originalText:
jictaskarin-da men an-da-gur3-ru
jicasal2-gin7 an-da-tal2-tal2 sal-la
hur-saj-da an-da-sig7-sig7-ga
chunks.translatedText:
With boxwood crown it stands aloft, a glory to behold,
Like poplars spreading wide and far, its branches manifold,
As verdant as the mountain range, in beauty yet untold.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-an-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 4-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The fourth house.

chapters.notes: This section describes the temple as a bustling city and a site of ritual perfection, using animal and nature imagery to convey its majesty.

chapters.title: House 5
chapters.sourceText:
e2 pirij u3-tud cag4-bi ur-saj su3-ud
e2 kecki pirij u3-tud cag4-bi ur-saj su3-ud
cag4-bi ur-saj-ur-saj-e-ne si mu-un-si-sa2
dnin-hur-saj-ja2 ucum-gal-am3 cag4 im-mi-in-tuc
dnin-tud ama gal-la tud-tud mu-un-ja2-ja2

dacac7-gi4 ur-saj mezej mu-un-gu7-e
duru3-mac nijir-gal edin-na mu-un-da-an-til3
e2 lulim gu2-e am3-ma-gur-re
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu

nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 5-kam-ma

ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 5-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
e2 pirij u3-tud cag4-bi ur-saj su3-ud
e2 kecki pirij u3-tud cag4-bi ur-saj su3-ud
chunks.translatedText:
A house where lions are brought to birth, its heart a hero's pride,
The house of Kesh where lions are born, with heroes deep inside.

chunks.originalText: cag4-bi ur-saj-ur-saj-e-ne si mu-un-si-sa2
chunks.translatedText: Within its walls the heroes set all things in order there,

chunks.originalText: dnin-hur-saj-ja2 ucum-gal-am3 cag4 im-mi-in-tuc
chunks.translatedText: Ninhursag, the Great Dragon, sits within the sacred air.

chunks.originalText: dnin-tud ama gal-la tud-tud mu-un-ja2-ja2
chunks.translatedText: Nintud, the Great Mother, brings the births to light and life,

chunks.originalText: dcul-pa-ed2 ensi2-ke4 nam-en mu-un-na-ak
chunks.translatedText: Shulpae, the administrator, rules as lord and priest,

chunks.originalText: dacac7-gi4 ur-saj mezej mu-un-gu7-e
chunks.translatedText: Ashgi, the hero, eats the fat of the sacrificial feast.

chunks.originalText: duru3-mac nijir-gal edin-na mu-un-da-an-til3
chunks.translatedText: Urumash, the great herald, dwells upon the open plain,

chunks.originalText: e2 lulim gu2-e am3-ma-gur-re
chunks.translatedText: The house is like a stag that bellows loud across the main.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 5-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The fifth house.

chapters.notes: This section focuses on the divine inhabitants and the birthing power of the temple, identifying it as a place where lions are born and heroes dwell.

chapters.title: House 6
chapters.sourceText:
e2 lu2 10-e 10-e nam-mi-in-tuc
e2 kecki-ka lu2 10-e 10-e nam-mi-in-tuc
lugal-bi ucumgal-am3 an-na dirig-ga
nin-bi dnin-hur-saj-ja2-am3 gu2 ki-ce3 ma-al
e2 ensi2-bi dacac7-gi4-am3
nu-gig-bi dnin-tud-ra
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 6-kam-ma

originalDocument.fullText:
e2 lu2 10-e 10-e nam-mi-in-tuc
e2 kecki-ka lu2 10-e 10-e nam-mi-in-tuc
lugal-bi ucumgal-am3 an-na dirig-ga
nin-bi dnin-hur-saj-ja2-am3 gu2 ki-ce3 ma-al
e2 ensi2-bi dacac7-gi4-am3
nu-gig-bi dnin-tud-ra

ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 6-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
e2 lu2 10-e 10-e nam-mi-in-tuc
e2 kecki-ka lu2 10-e 10-e nam-mi-in-tuc
chunks.translatedText:
The house where ten by ten the people dwell within its gate,
The house of Kesh where ten by ten the people congregate.

chunks.originalText:
lugal-bi ucumgal-am3 an-na dirig-ga
nin-bi dnin-hur-saj-ja2-am3 gu2 ki-ce3 ma-al
chunks.translatedText:
Its king is like a dragon great, who floats across the sky,
Its lady is Ninhursag, who to earth bows low and nigh.

chunks.originalText:
e2 ensi2-bi dacac7-gi4-am3
nu-gig-bi dnin-tud-ra
chunks.translatedText:
The governor of this great house is Ashgi, bold and strong,
Its high priestess is Nintud, to whom all births belong.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 6-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The sixth house.

chapters.notes: This section describes the inhabitants and divine hierarchy of the temple, emphasizing the sheer number of people (ten by ten) and the celestial and earthly roles of its deities.

chapters.title: House 7
chapters.sourceText:
e2 u3-tud kug-ga cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 kecki u3-tud kug-ga cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 caj4-mac-bi a-da-ab-ba
al-jar-bi kuccem3-am3
tigi-bi cem5-ma-am3
za-am-za-am-bi gi-di-da-am3
e2 nar-bi u3-ma-am3
sipa-bi ud-da-am3
e2 lu2 tigi-ba nar mu-un-tar-re
zam-zam-ma u3-ma mu-un-tar-re
e2 a-tu5-a-ba gud huc-am3
luh-luh-ha-ba ud-gin7 car2-ra-am3
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 7-kam-ma

originalDocument.fullText:
e2 u3-tud kug-ga cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 kecki u3-tud kug-ga cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 caj4-mac-bi a-da-ab-ba
al-jar-bi kuccem3-am3
tigi-bi cem5-ma-am3
za-am-za-am-bi gi-di-da-am3
e2 nar-bi u3-ma-am3
sipa-bi ud-da-am3
e2 lu2 tigi-ba nar mu-un-tar-re
zam-zam-ma u3-ma mu-un-tar-re
e2 a-tu5-a-ba gud huc-am3
luh-luh-ha-ba ud-gin7 car2-ra-am3
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 7-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
e2 u3-tud kug-ga cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
e2 kecki u3-tud kug-ga cag4-bi a-ba mu-zu
chunks.translatedText:
The house of holy birth—who knows its inner heart?
The house of Kesh, of holy birth—who knows its inner heart?

chunks.originalText:
e2 caj4-mac-bi a-da-ab-ba
al-jar-bi kuccem3-am3
tigi-bi cem5-ma-am3
za-am-za-am-bi gi-di-da-am3
chunks.translatedText:
The house's wild boar is the adab-song,
Its algar-lyre is the drum's deep beat,
Its tigi-drums are the tambourines,
Its zam-zam instruments are the reed-pipes sweet.

chunks.originalText:
e2 nar-bi u3-ma-am3
sipa-bi ud-da-am3
e2 lu2 tigi-ba nar mu-un-tar-re
zam-zam-ma u3-ma mu-un-tar-re
chunks.translatedText:
The house's singer is a triumph song,
Its shepherd is the light of day,
The house where tigi-players lead the choir,
And zam-zam songs of victory hold sway.

chunks.originalText:
e2 a-tu5-a-ba gud huc-am3
luh-luh-ha-ba ud-gin7 car2-ra-am3
chunks.translatedText:
Within its lustration room, a fierce bull stands,
Its cleansing rites are bright as sunlight's glow.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 7-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The seventh house.

chapters.notes: This section focuses on the musical and ritual life within the temple, highlighting the instruments and the divine atmosphere of the ceremonies.

chapters.title: House 8
chapters.sourceText:
an-ki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 kecki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 dacac7-gi4-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab

kecki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab

ud-ba asil-la2 te-ma-ab
itima-ba asil-la2 te-ma-ab

ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 8-kam-ma

e2 zi-da asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 dingir-re-ne asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 kug-ga asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 me-te-na asil-la2 te-ma-ab
kecki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
ud-de3 nam-lugal-la asil-la2 te-ma-ab
iti6-e nam-ensi2-ka asil-la2 te-ma-ab
mu-e nam-nin-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
ud-ba asil-la2 te-ma-ab
itima-ba asil-la2 te-ma-ab
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
e2 8-kam-ma

chunks.originalText:
an-ki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 kecki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 dacac7-gi4-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 dnin-hur-saj-ja2-ka asil-la2 te-ma-ab
chunks.translatedText:
In heaven and earth, let there be joy,
In the house of Kesh, let there be joy,
In the house of Ashgi, let there be joy,
In the house of Ninhursag, let there be joy.

chunks.originalText:
an-ki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 kecki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 nam-ti-la asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 zi-da asil-la2 te-ma-ab
chunks.translatedText:
In heaven and earth, let there be joy,
In the house of Kesh, let there be joy,
In the house of life, let there be joy,
In the faithful house, let there be joy.

chunks.originalText:
e2 dingir-re-ne asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 kug-ga asil-la2 te-ma-ab
e2 me-te-na asil-la2 te-ma-ab
kecki-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
chunks.translatedText:
In the house of the gods, let there be joy,
In the holy house, let there be joy,
In the house of fitting praise, let there be joy,
In Kesh itself, let there be joy.

chunks.originalText:
ud-de3 nam-lugal-la asil-la2 te-ma-ab
iti6-e nam-ensi2-ka asil-la2 te-ma-ab
mu-e nam-nin-a asil-la2 te-ma-ab
chunks.translatedText:
In the days of kingship, let there be joy,
In the months of lordship, let there be joy,
In the years of queenship, let there be joy.

chunks.originalText:
ud-ba asil-la2 te-ma-ab
itima-ba asil-la2 te-ma-ab
chunks.translatedText:
In its daylight, let there be joy,
In its inner chambers, let there be joy.

chunks.originalText:
kecki-gin7 rib-ba lu2 ci-in-ga-tum2-mu
ur-saj-bi dacac7-gi4-gin7 rib-ba ama ci-in-ga-u3-tud
nin-bi dnin-tud-gin7 rib-ba-ra a-ba-a igi mu-ni-in-du8
chunks.translatedText:
Will any man bring forth a thing so great as Kesh has been?
Will any mother bear a hero like Ashgi, so bold?
Who has seen a lady like Nintud, of beauty untold?

chunks.originalText: e2 8-kam-ma
chunks.translatedText: The eighth house.

chapters.notes: The final house of the Kesh Temple Hymn serves as a grand doxology, calling for joy throughout the cosmos and the temple, while repeating the refrain regarding the uniqueness of Kesh, its hero Ashgi, and its goddess Nintud.
